title: tetratheca hirsuta subsp. boonanarring
intro: Tetratheca hirsuta subsp. boonanarring is a rare sub-species of the hairy pink-pea, a flowering plant native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used in Indigenous Australian medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. The leaves and flowers of Tetratheca hirsuta subsp. boonanarring can be used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and poultices, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
